intro: The most concrete split between Alter and Bearer is the pricing model: Alter runs on custom pricing only (no public tier), while Bearer uses freemium. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Alter carries low lock-in (proprietary agent identity platform with custom protocols) and custom pricing only (no public tier); Bearer carries high lock-in (oss data security scanner) and freemium. Alter is a fit when you are mobile. Bearer is a fit when you are mobile. The honest trade-off: Alter is explicitly not for hobby projects; Bearer is explicitly not for hobby projects. On the product side, Alter is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Agent Identity, and Bearer is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Data Flow Security.
quick_take: Alter is for mobile; Bearer is for mobile; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Alter if its custom pricing model fits your budget shape better than Bearer's freemium model, and you are mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Bearer if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches mobile.

faq.a: Bearer has a free tier; Alter does not. For small-scale use Bearer will be cheaper. At paid scale the pricing models differ and the comparison depends on your volume.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Alter to Bearer?
faq.a: Migration paths between Alter and Bearer depend on the specific data and integrations. Neither vendor publishes a ready import tool for the other. Expect a custom export-transform-import exercise.

On our scoring Bearer rates 4/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Alter. That suggests Bearer feels more polished for everyday developer tasks. We still recommend a short hands-on trial on your stack before standardising.

faq.q: Is Bearer a good alternative to Alter?
faq.a: Bearer targets the same best-fit audience as Alter (mobile). Note that Bearer is explicitly not for hobby projects, so it only replaces Alter if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (low vs high), which is the main thing to weigh.
